scope: FRONTEND only (vitest/tsc/next build) — no backend pytest run, per brief

evidence: apps/web/src/app/dashboard/networking/page.tsx:114-140 saveContact(): calls createNetworkingContact(), then awaits fetchNetworkingSummary() and only closes the modal (setShowAdd(false)) after that resolves; catch block sets formError from e.message and does NOT call fetchNetworkingSummary or close the modal — no optimistic/fake success path exists.

apps/web/src/lib/api/workspaces.ts:87-104 createNetworkingContact() POSTs to '/networking/contacts' with body {name, title, company, email, linkedin_url, stage} — matches apps/api/app/routers/networking.py ContactCreate (name, title, company, email, linkedin_url, stage) exactly, and app/main.py:200 mounts networking.router at prefix '/networking' so POST /networking/contacts is the real route.

apps/web/src/lib/api/workspaces.ts:106-108 fetchNetworkingContact(id) → GET /networking/contacts/{id}, matching networking.py get_contact() and its _CONTACT_COLUMNS (id/userId/name/title/company/stage/email/linkedinUrl/createdAt/updatedAt) — the NetworkingContactRecord TS interface field names match the SQL column aliases 1:1.

Re-ran the 3 MV-networking-001 vitest cases against the PARENT commit (3eee0448, pre-fix page.tsx) in an isolated worktree: all 3 fail (calls-API test, refetch-after-success test, and the failure/honest-error test) — confirming they exercise real pre-fix behavior, not tautologies. Same 3 tests pass against c95e083.

apps/api/app/routers/workspaces.py:272-301 networking_summary() builds queue/log entries with exactly those keys (id/kind/status/contactName/company/subject/scheduledAt/sentAt) from real OutreachTask rows — frontend and backend shapes now match field-for-field.

page.tsx:284-339 Outreach Queue / Communication Log cards read o.contactName/o.company/o.subject/o.kind/o.status/o.scheduledAt and l.contactName/l.subject/l.kind/l.sentAt — no reference to the old nonexistent fields remains.

vitest MV-networking-002 cases assert queue/log text contains 'Mark K.', 'Canva', 'Follow Up — Canva', 'pending' and NOT the literal string 'undefined' — these failed against pre-fix code (verified) and pass post-fix.

page.tsx:183-195 empty state now reads 'Start building your recruiter & referral network by adding a contact manually...' with a single 'Add contact manually' button (data-testid empty-state-add-contact-btn); the old 'Import from LinkedIn' button/copy and 'Import your LinkedIn connections' claim are gone.

grep confirms no remaining 'Import from LinkedIn' / 'Import your LinkedIn connections' string in page.tsx post-fix; the design/screens/networking.html wireframe still shows the aspirational LinkedIn copy, but there is no LinkedIn OAuth backend anywhere in apps/api, so the relabel is the honest choice, consistent with this MV effort's prior honesty-over-wireframe rulings.
vitest MV-networking-003 cases (queryByText(/import from linkedin/i) is null; body text does not match /import your linkedin connections/i) failed pre-fix, pass post-fix.
page.tsx:233-246 contact cards are role="button" tabIndex={0} with onClick and onKeyDown (Enter/Space) handlers calling setSelectedContactId(c.id) — clickable and keyboard-activatable.

page.tsx:69-91 useEffect fetches fetchNetworkingContact(selectedContactId) → GET /networking/contacts/{id}; loading/error/body states rendered honestly (contact-detail-loading / contact-detail-error / contact-detail-body).

vitest MV-networking-005 case clicks a card, asserts fetchNetworkingContactMock called with 'c-1', and that the modal shows the fetched name/email/company — failed pre-fix (feature didn't exist), passes post-fix.

CONCERN (non-blocking): no automated test exercises the Enter/Space keyboard path specifically (only click is tested) — verified manually by code read, not by a failing/passing keyboard-specific test.

other_findings.MV-networking-004_dead_button_removed: PASS — 'Review all drafts' button block fully deleted from page.tsx (not disabled, not left as no-op); confirmed via diff. NOTE: the accompanying vitest case is a soft conditional (`if (reviewButton) { expect(...disabled).toBe(true) }`) that would vacuously pass even for an unrelated change — it does not itself assert absence. I independently confirmed removal via the diff/full file read, so the functional claim holds despite the weak test.
other_findings.MV-networking-006_stage_badge: PASS (code-verified) — page.tsx:260-266 renders a data-testid="contact-stage-badge" span with col.stage text on every card. CONCERN: no dedicated vitest assertion for this badge's presence/text; verified by direct file read only.
other_findings.MV-networking-009_cancel_resets_form: PASS — closeAddModal() resets form to EMPTY_FORM and clears formError; wired to both Cancel and the X button. vitest case fails pre-fix, passes post-fix.
other_findings.MV-networking-010_escape_closes: PASS — document-level keydown listener (page.tsx:103-112) closes whichever modal is open regardless of focus. vitest case for the Add-Contact modal fails pre-fix, passes post-fix. CONCERN: no dedicated test for Escape closing the contact-detail modal specifically, though the same handler covers both (verified by code read).

tests_meaningful.fail_before_pass_after: Re-ran the fix's own page.test.tsx + lib.test.ts against parent commit 3eee0448 (pre-fix page.tsx/lib.ts/workspaces.ts) in an isolated git worktree with a real pnpm install (not a symlink into the live repo). Result: 16/24 failed pre-fix (11 of the 11 page.test.tsx behavioral cases relevant to MV-networking-001/002/003/004/005/009/010 failed, plus 5 lib.test.ts cases for the brand-new formatOutreachKind/formatWhen helpers that don't exist pre-fix) — exactly matching the fixer's claimed '11 fail-before' for the page-level suite. All 24 pass against c95e083.
tests_meaningful.note: Not tautological — assertions check concrete rendered text, mock call args/counts, DOM presence/absence of testids, and disabled/empty states, not just 'no exception thrown'.

vitest_result.next_build: clean; route /dashboard/networking compiles and prerenders as static (○) at 5.77 kB / 93.1 kB First Load JS
vitest_result.method: All four checks run against commit c95e083 in an isolated `git worktree add` copy with an independent `pnpm install --frozen-lockfile` (not a symlink into the live repo's node_modules, to avoid corrupting the live checkout) — command outputs captured directly from this run.

prohibited_patterns_checked: eslint-disable — one instance, apps/web/.../__tests__/page.test.tsx: `// eslint-disable-next-line import/first` immediately above the mocked-component import (required because vi.mock hoisting forces the import after the mock call). Confirmed this exact convention already exists in 8 other test files in the repo (grep). Test-file-only, does not suppress a production error — NOT treated as a violation.
any casts (': any' / 'as any') — none found in diff
Math.random() metrics — none found in diff
TODO/FIXME stubs reachable by users — none found in diff
suppressed errors (bare except/catch swallowing) — none; the one catch block (saveContact) surfaces the error to the user via formError, does not swallow it
--no-verify / force-push — not applicable, no git operations in this diff review

notes: apps/web/src/app/dashboard/page.tsx (the only other consumer of NetworkingSummary) only reads crmSummary, whose shape is unchanged by this diff — no breakage from the outreachQueue/communicationLog type change.

totalContacts()/buildPipelineColumns() in lib.ts retain a now-vestigial 'added: NetworkingContact[]' parameter (always called with `[]` from page.tsx since the old local-optimistic-add state was correctly removed for MV-networking-001) and a doc comment describing 'contacts added locally in this session' that no longer happens — pre-existing code from an earlier fix cycle, not introduced by this diff, but now stale/misleading. Flagged as a cleanup concern, not a functional defect.

concerns: Contact-detail panel renders the raw backend stage enum (e.g. 'identified') via DetailRow, while the pipeline board shows the mapped label ('New') for the same contact — a minor label inconsistency between two honest-but-differently-formatted views of the same field. Not dishonest (both are real backend values), just not normalized to match.

lib.ts's buildPipelineColumns/totalContacts keep a stale 'added contacts' merge parameter and doc comment that no longer reflects actual page.tsx behavior (always passed []) — pre-existing debt, not newly introduced, but should be cleaned up in a follow-up since it could mislead a future reader into thinking local-add-then-persist demo behavior still exists.

Three sub-findings (MV-networking-004's button-removal, MV-networking-006's stage badge, and Escape-closing the contact-detail modal specifically) lack dedicated/strict automated assertions — the MV-004 test is a soft conditional that would vacuously pass on an unrelated change, and MV-006 / detail-modal-Escape have no test at all. I verified all three by direct code read against the committed diff, so the functional claims hold, but test coverage for them is weaker than for the other findings in this cluster.

Backend follow-ups MV-networking-007 (OutreachTask FK/cascade) and MV-networking-008 (emailAgent integration) are explicitly out of this frontend-only diff's scope per the commit message and the review brief — correctly not attempted here, flagging only so they aren't lost track of.
reasons: PASS: MV-networking-001 root cause (client-side-only fake Add-Contact state that vanished on reload) is fixed at the source — saveContact() now calls the real POST /networking/contacts and only reflects success after a GET /workspaces/networking/summary refetch confirms server-side persistence; failure path is honest (error shown, modal stays open, no refetch).
PASS: endpoint paths/shapes verified line-for-line against apps/api/app/routers/networking.py and apps/api/app/routers/workspaces.py — no mismatch.
PASS: MV-networking-002 field-mismatch fixed with an exact match to the real backend response shape; verified by diff + live backend router code.
PASS: MV-networking-003 LinkedIn claim removed, replaced with an accurate label for what the button actually does.

PASS: MV-networking-005/004/006/009/010 all implemented and code-verified; test coverage is present for 001/002/003/005/009/010's primary paths and thinner (or absent) for a few sub-details noted above.

PASS: fail-before/pass-after discipline independently reproduced in an isolated worktree — not taking the fixer's word for it. 11/11 relevant page.test.tsx cases fail against parent commit 3eee0448, pass against c95e083.
PASS: full vitest (341/341), tsc --noEmit (clean), and next build (clean, route prerenders) independently re-run against c95e083 in this session, not copied from the commit message.

PASS: no prohibited patterns (placeholders, suppressed errors, broad any, ts-ignore beyond the pre-existing repo-wide eslint-disable convention, Math.random metrics, fake success) found anywhere in the diff.
No regression found in the one other consumer of the changed types (dashboard/page.tsx).
